title: Only check for PodStateExited when pruning pods
body:
[NO TESTS NEEDED] This is an attempt to fix a Race condition
since it is a race it is difficult to ~~fix~~ test.

Helps fix: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/7139

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.
-->

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

I'll keep my fingers crossed.

I'm seeing a HUGE number of flakes in the `podman start --all` test, which has nothing to do with pods, so I think there might be two separate issues. This one LGTM though.
The --start=all is brand new, so I can take a look tomorrow,

I'm not sure it has to do with `start --all`. My working hypothesis, which I'll start work on tomorrow, is that it has to do with `podman rm cid1 cid2 cid3` (a list of multiple CIDs). But it could also be related to `rmi` of a container that has been restarted. Anyhow, more tomorrow. Thanks for this one.
I think my other PR will fix all of them.
